summary.complaint: 42-year-old male presenting with excessive daytime sleepiness (EDS) of 6 months duration. Epworth Sleepiness Scale score 16/24 (pathological). Spouse reports loud snoring with witnessed apneic episodes occurring 4-5 times per night. Patient describes unrefreshing sleep despite adequate sleep duration (7-8 hours). Associated morning headaches 3-4 times per week. No narcolepsy features. Significant caffeine dependence (3-4 cups/day).
summary.pastHistory: Known case of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (diagnosed 2019) on Metformin 500mg BD. Hypertension (diagnosed 2020) on Telmisartan 40mg OD. No surgical history. No prior sleep studies. Positive family history: Father diagnosed with OSA at age 55; Mother with Type 2 DM. No history of alcohol use.
summary.vitals: BP: 142/88 mmHg (Stage 1 Hypertension — suboptimal control on current medication). HR: 78 bpm (regular). SpO2: 94% on room air (borderline low at rest — concerning for baseline hypoxemia). Temperature: 98.4°F (afebrile). Weight: 92 kg, Height: 170 cm, BMI: 31.8 kg/m² (Obese Class I, WHO classification).
summary.physicalExamination: General: Obese male, no acute distress. ENT: Mallampati Grade III (high risk for upper airway collapse during sleep). Neck circumference 42 cm (exceeds 40 cm threshold — elevated OSA risk). Retrognathia noted (anatomical predisposition for OSA). Tonsils Grade 1 — no hypertrophy. Chest: Clear bilaterally, no adventitious sounds. CVS: S1S2 normal, no murmurs. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, centrally obese. CNS: Alert, oriented, no focal deficits.
summary.investigationOrdered:
1. Level 1 Polysomnography (PSG) — COMPLETED at Sleep Lab SL-MUMBAI-003:
   - AHI: 34.2 events/hour (Severe OSA, threshold >30)
   - Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI): 28 events/hour
   - Minimum SpO2: 78% (significant nocturnal desaturation)
   - Sleep Efficiency: 68.5% (reduced, normal >85%)
   - REM AHI: 52.1 events/hour (markedly elevated during REM)
   - Supine AHI: 48.3 events/hour (significant positional component)
2. Thyroid Function Test — COMPLETED (results pending in report)
3. HbA1c — COMPLETED (results pending in report)
summary.diagnosis:
1. Severe Obstructive Sleep Apnea (AHI 34.2 events/hr) — ICD-10: G47.33
2. Obesity Class I (BMI 31.8 kg/m²) — ICD-10: E66.01
3. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, controlled — ICD-10: E11.65
4. Essential Hypertension, Stage 1, suboptimal control — ICD-10: I10
5. Excessive Daytime Sleepiness secondary to OSA — ICD-10: G47.10
summary.advice:
1. Initiate CPAP therapy at 10 cmH2O (auto-titrating CPAP recommended given severe AHI and significant positional/REM variation)
2. CPAP titration study to be scheduled at SL-MUMBAI-003 within 2 weeks
3. Weight reduction program: Target BMI <30 (lose ~5 kg). Refer to dietitian.
4. Sleep hygiene counseling: Avoid supine sleeping position (elevated supine AHI 48.3). Use positional therapy device if tolerated.
5. Review antihypertensive: Consider uptitrating Telmisartan to 80mg OD or adding Amlodipine 5mg for better BP control (target <130/80 mmHg per AHA guidelines for comorbid DM)
6. Driving safety advisory: Counsel patient regarding drowsy driving risk given ESS 16/24 and occupation requiring 45-min daily commute. Consider temporary driving restriction until CPAP compliance established.
7. Follow-up in 4 weeks with: CPAP compliance data (target >4 hours/night on >70% nights), repeat SpO2 monitoring, TFT and HbA1c results review
8. Sulfonamide allergy noted — avoid sulfa-containing medications in any future prescriptions
